status: DEV — clinician_review pending; NOT patient-facing; reference-only (engine-isolated). Consumer counselling messages (APF22 Consumer information structure). Records author to review_status 'draft'; require clinician verification against source + AMH/TGA.

attestation.statement: counselling_points reference register — APF22-cited facts / public primary sources; NOT prescribing, NOT a dose source.

records.point: Complete the full course even if you feel better
Take on an empty stomach with a full glass of water and stay upright for at least 30 minutes
Take with or after food to reduce stomach upset
Keep your vitamin K intake consistent and report any unusual bleeding or bruising
Seek medical advice if you need your reliever more often than usual
Report unexplained muscle pain, tenderness or weakness

For inflammatory conditions this medicine is taken ONCE A WEEK, not every day. Taking it daily can cause severe, potentially fatal toxicity. Confirm the day of the week and the once-weekly schedule at every dispense.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)

records.point: Keep your intake of vitamin-K foods (green leafy vegetables) steady from week to week rather than avoiding them, take your dose at the same time each day, and keep your INR blood-test appointments.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)

records.point: It can take 2-4 weeks to feel the full benefit. Do not stop suddenly, as this can cause withdrawal effects; talk to your prescriber before making any change.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)

records.point: Your skin may burn more easily in sunlight while taking this medicine; use sun protection and avoid prolonged sun exposure.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)

records.point: Do not stop this medicine suddenly if you have been taking it for more than a couple of weeks; the dose usually needs to be reduced gradually. Carry a steroid alert card.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)

records.point: If you need your blue reliever inhaler more often than usual (for example, more than a few times a week for symptoms), your asthma may not be well controlled; arrange a review.

provenance.source: APF22 (facts, cited) — Section D Consumer information / Counselling
provenance.authored_by: claude-chat clinical review (APF22-cited facts; no dose amounts)
